ops.text: Shared-working-copy .venv race in captain-hook: the docs dependency group (great-docs, griffe) gets silently pruned from the shared .venv whenever a concurrent session runs a plain `uv sync`, so any local docs-build invocation relying on the venv alone flakes with ModuleNotFoundError mid-baseline. The CI-shaped `uv run --with "git+...#subdirectory=tools/gd-build" gd-build build` form is self-sufficient and survives the prune; a bare `.venv`-based `great-docs build` or import check does not. Cost us one failed cold-build attempt (UNPATCHED: shared-griffe-loader → stock → import error) before diagnosing. Every local docs-build instruction should mandate the --with form and never trust `.venv` persistence between steps.
